##NAMES=Ferrobobfergusonite
##RRUFFID=R140993
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=[box]Na_2_Fe^2+^_5_Fe^3+^Al(PO_4_)_6_
##LOCALITY=Victory mine, Custer, Custer District, Custer County, South Dakota, USA
##OWNER=RRUFF
##SOURCE=Rock Currier
##DESCRIPTION=Dark green aggregates of blocky crystals, associated with quartz and muscovite. Additionally, four different dark green minerals are found in the deposit that can be confused with ferrobobfergusonite: schorl, fillowite, ferrobobfergusonite, and ferrowyllieite. The light green minerals are arrojadite. This is the type sample
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R140993
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Na_1.73_Ca_0.13_)(Fe^2+^_3.51_Mn_0.89_Mg_0.43_)(Fe^3+^_0.77_Al_0.23_)Al(P_1.01_O_4_)_6_
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 12.7156 b: 12.3808 c: 10.9347 alpha: 90 beta: 97.3329 gamma: 90 volume: 1707.37 crystal system: monoclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman Processed
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
